findings.title: No regression test for lowercase/all-lowercase temp view names (the pre-fix happy path)
findings.why_it_matters: The fix changes `temp_views` to `temp_views_lower` in the guard inside `_lookup`. The existing tests (e.g. `test_temp_view_resolution`, `test_databricks_sql_notebook`) all use already-lowercase view names so they still pass whether the bug is present or not. They do not distinguish the old code from the new. If someone accidentally reverts or double-lowers `temp_views_lower`, the new test catches it but neither old test does. A complementary test that explicitly names a view in lowercase and verifies it resolves would make the intent unambiguous and the regression surface symmetric.
evidence: All pre-existing temp-view tests create views with all-lowercase names (e.g. `stg`, `staging`, `step1`, `step2`).

The fixed line is `if src_tbl not in temp_views_lower` (line 739) â€” the old code used `temp_views` (the original mixed-case set). Passing a lowercase name succeeds with both old and new code, so pre-existing tests cannot detect a revert.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test that names the view in lowercase (e.g. `my_staging` without uppercasing) and asserts it resolves correctly â€” this gives a baseline counterpart to the new uppercase test.

findings.title: New test does not cover the wildcard path through `_lookup` with a mixed-case view name
findings.why_it_matters: The new test uses explicit named columns (`id`, `val`) so `_lookup` takes the direct key-match branch (`if src in tv_sources`). The wildcard-expansion branch (`if src.endswith('.*')`) and the named-column-with-wildcard-fallback branch (`wildcard_sources = tv_sources.get(f'{src_tbl}.*')`) are both reachable when a temp view is created via `SELECT *`. If those paths also rely on the fixed `temp_views_lower` guard, a regression there would go undetected by the new test.
evidence: `_lookup` at line 741 checks `if src.endswith('.*')` after the fixed guard â€” the guard at line 739 gates all three branches, so the fix protects them, but no test exercises the wildcard branches with a mixed-case view name.
The wildcard expansion path (lines 742-751) and wildcard-fallback path (lines 752-761) are functionally important and are only tested by pre-existing lowercase-view tests.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Databricks notebook test where `CREATE OR REPLACE TEMPORARY VIEW MY_STAGING AS SELECT * FROM source_tbl` (SELECT *) is consumed by `INSERT INTO final SELECT * FROM MY_STAGING`, and assert that final traces back to source_tbl columns via the wildcard path.

findings.title: New test assertion on `sources` set uses `.lower()` transformation that hides if casing is wrong in emitted edges
findings.why_it_matters: The test builds `sources = {e.source_col.lower() for e in edges}` and `targets = {e.target_col.lower() for e in edges}`. If the parser accidentally emits `UC_DEV.RAW.SOURCE_TBL.ID` (upper-cased) instead of the normalized lowercase form, the assertion still passes because it lowercases both. The test therefore does not verify that the edge IDs conform to the all-lowercase normalization invariant documented in CLAUDE.md.
evidence: CLAUDE.md states: 'All column IDs are catalog.schema.table.column (4-part)' with no mention of allowed case variation, implying lowercase is the canonical form.
The test asserts `'uc_dev.raw.source_tbl.id' in sources` where `sources` is already lowercased â€” the assertion would pass even if `e.source_col == 'UC_DEV.RAW.SOURCE_TBL.ID'`.
findings.suggested_fix: Assert directly on `{e.source_col for e in edges}` (without `.lower()`) to verify the emitted IDs are already lowercase, matching the canonical form. Keep a separate check for the non-leakage of `MY_STAGING`.

findings.title: Chain resolution loop (lines 763-782) checks `src_tbl in temp_views_lower` but iterates over already-lowercased `tv_sources` keys â€” mixed-case chained view names could still fail
findings.why_it_matters: The chain-resolution loop at line 771 checks `if src_tbl in temp_views_lower` using a lowercased `src_tbl` derived from the already-lowercased `tv_sources` key, so this path is safe. However, if `temp_views` is populated from a caller that preserves original casing (e.g. `MY_STAGING` remains in `temp_views` as-is), the original guard before the fix (`if src_tbl not in temp_views`) inside `_lookup` would have failed â€” this is exactly what the fix addresses. No test exercises a chained-view scenario where any link in the chain has a mixed-case name, so the fix is untested in the chain context.
evidence: `temp_views` is passed in as a `set[str]` from the caller and is not normalized before `_resolve_temp_views` is entered â€” normalization happens inside the function at line 717.
The existing chained-view test (`test_temp_view_chained`) uses lowercase `step1`/`step2`, so a revert of the fix would not be caught for the chained case.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a chained-temp-view test where intermediate views use mixed case (e.g. `CREATE TEMP VIEW STEP_ONE AS ... CREATE TEMP VIEW STEP_TWO AS SELECT ... FROM STEP_ONE ... INSERT INTO final SELECT ... FROM STEP_TWO`) and assert full resolution to the base table.

residual_risks: The fix is a one-line change (`temp_views` â†’ `temp_views_lower`) in a single branch of `_lookup`. All other branches of `_lookup` and the chain-resolution loop already operate on lowercased keys derived from `tv_sources`, so the fix is correctly scoped. No unintended side-effects were identified.

SQLGlot (dialect=databricks) normalizes identifiers to lowercase during AST parsing; however, the temp_views set is populated before that normalization in some call paths, which is the root cause the fix addresses. If SQLGlot's normalization behavior changes for a future dialect version, `temp_views_lower` remains a safe guard.

The `_resolve_temp_views` function is not called directly in tests â€” it is exercised only through `parse_sql`. This means any future refactoring that moves the temp_views normalization step could silently break the fix without test failure.
testing_gaps: No test for mixed-case temp view name in the wildcard SELECT * path (both wildcard-expansion and wildcard-fallback branches of _lookup).
No test for mixed-case chained temp views (two or more hops where at least one intermediate view name is uppercase).
New test lowercases both sides of assertions, making it unable to catch canonical-casing violations in emitted edge IDs.

No counterpart baseline test that confirms all-lowercase view names (the pre-existing happy path) still resolve correctly after the change â€” the pre-existing tests serve this purpose incidentally but were not written with the normalization invariant in mind.
